prompt: Before we touch code, I want a few serious architecture options for a feature that can execute a saved plan, keep progress state, and coordinate code-review agents at the right checkpoints. It needs to work in this skills repo and build on the existing plan/task workflow without becoming a monolith. Please compare alternatives and tell me which direction you'd bet on.
assumptions: A saved plan means the normal scratchpad artifact set rooted at .agents/scratchpad/<feature>/, especially plan.md and tasks.md.
tasks.md remains the authoritative completion and resumption ledger; any new execution state is derived and must reconcile back to tasks.md.
Checkpoint reviews should reuse the existing code-simplifier and code-reviewer agents and preserve the current controller-owned review-scope rules.
Durable progress state should live next to scratchpad artifacts, not in workspace outputs or a new database layer.
